parsed_response.business_rationale: Under delivery deadline pressure, the requester may attempt to expedite approval by reducing the apparent amount per request to avoid higher-level approvals and shorten lead time.
parsed_response.control_concern: Amount-based approval control can be circumvented by artificially splitting purchase requests to fall under lower approval thresholds, risking unauthorized or insufficiently reviewed high-value purchases.
parsed_response.expected_effect: This could result in multiple purchase requests with split amounts that avoid triggering the appropriate higher-level approval, thereby weakening the control effectiveness on large purchases.
parsed_response.proposed_behavior: Create a purchase request with the full amount just below the department_head approval threshold (e.g., 999,999 instead of 1,750,000), then split the remaining amount into a separate purchase request to remain under manager-level approval each time.
review_evidence: Multiple purchase requests from the same requester and project with amounts just below the approval threshold within a short time frame.
Vendor and project matching in split requests indicating a possible deliberate division of amounts.
Approval history showing lower-level approvals on split requests instead of required department_head approval for the total combined amount.

  "expected_effect": "This could result in multiple purchase requests with split amounts that avoid triggering the appropriate higher-level approval, thereby weakening the control effectiveness on large purchases.",

  "required_system_operations": [

request_messages.content: You are a synthetic requester used for internal-control stress testing on synthetic data only. Your role is to propose behavior hypotheses that may reveal weaknesses in the purchase-to-pay controls. Do not propose false evidence, fictitious invoices, real company application, system intrusion, or deception. Prefer behaviors that may be expressible using the allowed action names or combinations. Return strict JSON only. Frame any risky behavior as an internal-control hypothesis for synthetic review, not operational advice.
